user_comment: I wear a 46K bra (but usually have to squeeze into a 42G or 44F due to availability) and I have a horrible time finding supportive suits that provide both lift *and* coverage. This suit is not what I would call super supportive, BUT it holds the girls in well enough to not feel like I’m going to flash everyone, the print is SUPER cute, and it’s very comfortable. I’m so glad I got it! I was worried it would be too long, but luckily (unluckily lol) I have a belly and a booty so it makes it appear shorter. Highly recommend!! I would suggest sizing down so that it’s tighter in the chest area, that’s what I did!

I went into this knowing there would be no support. I have very large bra size and usually require wearing a bra under my bathing suits. First photo I have bra on and the next one I have no bra to show you the lack of support. Over all I love the suit. It’s super cute. Perfect sizing. I ordered 24. My bra size 42 I, 5’4” 275 lbs. Update- got my daughter the same suit. She is 5’4 and 330 and we got her the 28 and it was perfect.

The color is really a dark grey purple, which is what I thought by the picture and other comments. Not coffee, but that’s what I was hoping for. The top is super comfortable because it has no metal. It’s also pretty supportive. The bottoms run a little big, but the benefit is that I’ll be comfortable on the days of bloat. They also gather in the middle, in the back of the bottoms due to seam going down the middle (which is weird) making the material point out a bit. I will note, I have a long torso and I lost my bum after pregnancy. Overall, I really like the suit because it’s comfortable AND makes my post breastfeeding boobs look like they have some life. 5’4, C34, 110lbs and ordered a size small.

BUY IT! I’m a curvy girl who spent hours looking for a modest, flattering swimsuit and this was the one, two, and three. I was mainly worried about finding a swimsuit that would cover my booty and tatas since those are my more ~plentiful~ areas (I am a size 16 in jeans and a 38 D-DD in bra) while also not accentuating my belly. It covered all my bits and is comfortable, and flattering. AND the straps are adjustable 😭👏🏼 The tie did make it ride up a little too much for my taste, so I just undid the knot and tied one that didn’t make the fabric so short. I am 5’4, 215 Lb and got an XL in the brown color. This is absolutely perfect with a coverup as the cherry on top. Can’t speak for the durability but I don’t swim much so it’s perfect for a fun group gathering where I don’t want to flash anyone! This is the most confident I have felt in a swimsuit in a long time so I HIGHLY recommend!

The color blocking, and the way they shaped it is incredibly slimming because it brings the attention to the thinnest part of you which is your waist line. The zipper stayed up. I wore around my house for like 20 minutes and I moved and stuff and everything was good, it actually does have a lot of room in the torso which is gonna be really good if you’re taller. I will say that the placement of the cups isn’t super secure. They move a little bit and with this style top, you kinda want them to not be wandering, and it’s probably better for smaller to midsize breasts not really large chests with the way that cut of the side of the bathing suit is and the zipper. But it’s so flattering it has really good coverage in the booty a little cheeky, but still plenty of coverage. I really like this swimsuit I would recommend staying to size! I'm 5'5", 38 DDD, and 200 lbs. Shopping for swimwear that is flattering can be a challenge. This swimsuit did not disappoint. I LOVED that it cinches pretty tight on the waist and hugs your entire body. This is my first time purchasing from CUPSHE and will definitely be purchasing more swimwear from their company. I was concerned about the cleavage because I didn't want to look vulgar or be spilling all over the place. I was able to adjust the straps for a better fit. The color was a beautiful blue and I am very happy with my purchase. PROS: Strong material, great waist cinching, flattering for many body types, beautiful color CONS: The back is open somewhat. Although the straps are adjustable in the back if you have back fat it will show unfortunately. This is not a flaw in the design of the swimsuit but something to keep in mind if you are on the curvier or heavier side.

Super cute and flowy swimsuit cover up. Really pretty raspberry/ dark magenta color. Looks just like the photo. Better quality than expected. Fabric has a slight texture to it. Very lightweight, but not thin in a bad way. Love the wide V neckline. Can be worn off one shoulder. True to size. I am 5'4", 130 lbs, 34D bust, and I purchased size small. The only thing I will point out is it will shrink a good 6 inches. I washed it in the machine on cold, and laid it flat to dry. I would hate to see how small it would come out if it accidentally got put in the dryer! I am showing it post shrinking in the photo. It used to be right above the knee, and now it is quite short. That's alright for me though because I am kind of short and it is to cover up a swimsuit. If you are alright with a shorter cover up or plan on handwash only then I would recommend!

I was skeptical about buying this bathing suit after reading some bad reviews. I should have listened to my gut. I purchased this bathing suit after being invited on a last minute weekend vacation to a Waterpark. It arrived the night before I left, no complaints about the delivery. I tried the bathing suit on and was so excited. It looked just liked the pictures, appeared brand new, and the print! I adore the dinosaur print! I am a large chested girl (38DDD) and my breasts were mostly covered, besides a small bit of cleavage. The straps did dig a bit, but not enough to bother me. Overall, I was happy with my new purchase! After coming back to the hotel room on our first night at the Waterpark, I noticed something off about my bathing suit bottoms. The fabric looks as if... it got scratched by a cat? Imagine my disappointment after getting this bathing suit I love, just to have it look like this after wearing it once. I didn't ride any waterslides or do anything crazy. I was guiding my almost 2 year old through the kids areas most of the time. The only thing that I can imagine happened was I sat down in the pool with my baby. And a bathing suit should be able to withstand that. I would have been happy to pay the (not so low) price had this bathing suit lasted AT ALL. I am very disappointed that it didn't. I will be returning this immediately! 2 stars because I love the dinosaurs.

Ordered for and comments from my wife: I’m 5'7", 190lbs, with a 42" bust, and normally wear size 16-18 in suits or XXL sizes. I carry weight in my mid section. This was a little tight on me around the waist and top, but the size chart measurements were accurate. Goal is for me to shrink into this sooner than later.Material is soft and stretchy. I like that the use/care instructions for laundering are printed onto a tag that is thin plastic and seems waterproof. Like most all swimwear, cold water wash and air dry. For the top, the padded inserts are removable. The bottom is nicely lined as is the top. The built in bottom brief provided perfect coverage, and the slit of the skirt can be adjusted to be more open if desired. This is my suit of choice right now, and think this will be flattering for most all body types, but will be happier with it on my body after shedding a few pounds. Nice quality and would order again.

I'm usually leery about buying two things online: bathing suits and shoes. But this suit was such a great color and had such a promising design, I took a leap of faith. I'm so glad I went for this suit. It is incredibly well-made, with 50UV protective fabric and even double stitching (my grandmother was a Parisian dress designer who taught me to always pay attention to fabric quality and stitching--she'd have approved of this suit). I love the slenderizing design and the fact that it has the serious swimmer in mind. This is not a swimsuit that with fall off your shoulders when you are doing a butterfly stroke! And at the same time, I would not be embarrassed to be seen in this poolside, thanks to the wonderful green colors and the clever slenderizing panels. (I'm a 5'4" 140-145 lb 66 year old woman and when my husband saw me in it, he gave it two big thumbs up, and said that it was the best bathing suit color he'd seen on me). The only thing, a small thing, is that the dark green trim on the bust/top edge seems to pull down. It does not interfere with function,though, and may well stretch out over time. I am busty 38C, so that may be part of what is causing the issue. For reference, I bought a size L. I am so happy with this suit, and expect I will be buying another one like this when this one eventually wears out.

I have never tried to buy a swimsuit online, but for this price, this was worth a try for a future trip. The material and sewing is decent quality in bright shades of blue, is normal swimsuit thickness, with modesty bra panels sewed in. The attached photos show reality of the fit and look, unlike the photo-shopped ad. The size Medium fits okay when dry (5' 4", 120#); I wouldn't want it any tighter, but don't want it too loose before getting wet. The straps and back are a comfortable design so you don't worry about the suit falling or shifting with activity. The bottom rides up just a little before getting wet, the top trim and piping does not sit flat; could be a sewing issue or may vary with body shape.

Not feeling this one. My measurements were not ideal for this. The pants fit, the band size was way too big, the bust was just the right size but was sewn together poorly, and the shirt was too big. In other words if you're big endowed, this will not fit. The sewing was really messy and contributed to a sloppy look. The elastic was not attached securely in the chest band and had flipped around at some point during production. The seams in the front also randomly decided to flip sides. Since the bust didn't have any form or support to it, the top looked very blobby all around and made my breasts sit wide in a very unflattering way. The band was also so loose that in the process of taking the shirt on and off, I slipped out from underneath. This bra top is not suitable for anyone with a big difference in band and cup size (I'm a 38 H for reference) On the tank top there were these weird little stitches on either side of the breast which did nothing but make it look crumpled up. The pants fit fine, but don't have any kind of liner and since they're a bit loose at the leg I'd want to wear underwear or a bikini bottom under them (not included) The top was also see through. There is an extra layer to prevent seeing nip but you can clearly see the extra layer, the pads, and the tag. The fabric is very see through and water will only make this worse. Ultimately I feel like this would perform very poorly in the pool and I would look like a mess wearing it. The black ones might hide these design flaws better but overall this is a cheap poorly made product. I'll be returning if possible.

I loved this swimsuit from the pictures online because it looked long enough to cover everything and still be super cute. I was not disappointed! I am apple shaped with a bigger stomach and a smallish chest and usually swimsuits are either too short, the chest area is too big, the shorts that come with it don’t fit, or they just aren’t flattering. Well, this swimsuit was none of that! It fits amazingly like a short dress which sold me right out the gate. The chest area fits great, especially since the straps are adjustable. I may end up taking the padding out just because it doesn’t stay in place very well. The top is not too tight around my bust before opening up and flowing down and being very flattering to whatever shape your tummy is. I wasn’t even going to bother trying on the shorts it comes with, but I’m glad I did! They’re actually a good length so I can wear them over my stomach like usual and still have some material left for my legs. Personally I don’t mind short shorts or showing off my legs/skin so both pieces of this swimsuit are a win to me. I may end up buying another one in a different pattern! For sizing help, I purchased the 18 plus and my normal sizes are: 38-40C/L-XL bra; L-XL mens tops, XL-2XL womens tops; 16-18/XL-XXL pants/dresses

First, sizing: I’m 5’5”, a 34D, small or medium in tops depending on fit, and 8/10 in jeans. Based on the size chart I’d probably be a 3XL. Next, style: The material is extremely soft. Not what I was expecting for a cover-up but a very pleasant surprise (this could double as a regular dress)! The colors are vibrant. The tie on the waist is functional so I can tighten it. It arrived this morning and I’ve been wearing it since I tried it on, it’s THAT comfy. I’ll be ordering at least one more. I can see this as my go-to outfit working from home in the warm months in addition to wearing over swim suits.

I ordered this hoping to use it as a dress and it is PERFECT! Well, for me based on size and my height/proportions. I am 5'3, 130 lbs and ordered a small. I have average legs, average torso, so while it is short on me, it worked perfectly as a mini dress. (I just took care how I bent down if needed..lol)Fabric is SOFT, medium weight but not at all hot, very well constructed, not at all see-through. Have washed them all 2-3 times already (gentle cycle, hung dry), they still look brand new. The deep V on the back and bead detailing is also a nice touch. As other reviewers have said, where the draw-string to tie it is below the waist line, it still worked well for me and was very flattering on me regardless. I loved it so much I also bought in black, dark navy blue and the black dot. I LOVE these! Wore them all on a February trip to Maui and got tons of compliments! Buy a coordinating ruana, boho cover up or light sweater and it gets even more versatile! All four colors I received are exactly the same size, cut and fabric.One of my best Amazon clothing purchases ever!

I wanted an itty bitty bikini to tan in and thought this one would be great for that. Unfortunately, in order to get a bottom that doesn't dig into me, I would have to order an XL or bigger and then the top would be way too big. I ordered a L and the top is already bigger than I would like (about normal coverage, def not micro) but as you can see from my photo, the bottom is digging into me. For reference, my measurement where the bottom sits is 36" and I'm a C cup. I'm 5'5" and 130lbs. There's also very little stretch. The quality is also just mid. The fabric is thin. It's not terrible but I also wouldn't call it good or anywhere near what some of these other reviews say. It is definitely Amazon quality, not like the quality of a suit from an actual known brand. It's not see through (I mean, I think some of the colors are *meant* to be transparent but I got one of the ones that isn't meant to be see through) but I noticed that I could see the white tag through the bottom, which wouldn't be a problem if you removed it. The entire suit is unlined, so it's just the one layer of the thin fabric. I wanted to return it but ended up keeping it since trying it on is basically like wearing it due to the nature of the bottoms.

For perspective, I’m 5’7”, 125 lbs, and really flat chested with a decent shaped backside. I got the medium and it fit perfectly. Even though I’m really flat chested, I think the small would’ve actually been too small for me. Also, I was able to fit my gel inserts into the bra part (it does come with a small bra insert but that’s not enough for me). I loved this bathing suit when I first saw it and I love it still. Great quality, nice fabric, so cute, love the reverse bottom option, and the ties on the sides of the bikini bottoms and on the back of the bikini top are really cute. So flattering from the front but… did not personally work for my backside. No one, absolutely no one, needs to see that much of my @$$. It was riding up on me while I was just standing there taking these pics. I can’t imagine where it would’ve ended up after walking around the pool. I like my butt, and I don’t even care about the cellulite, but I’m personally not interested in putting all of my goods out there on display. You can see from my tan lines that my bikini bottoms typically cover a bit more. But that’s just my personal taste. I know the new styles are more cheeky… I just can’t do it. If you have a great tight, firm, and perky butt, you need to rock this suit because it’s adorable. If you like the cheeky styles, this suit is too cute. This girl, however, wants a little more butt coverage.

Very nice for hiding the mommy tummy or "muffin top". Not clingy in the stomach area and it lays nicely. The material isn't heavy like the other suits I like, but it isn't too light either. The sinches on the sides allow you to lengthen the top as well if you have a longer torso like mine. I am size 16ish, 14 is a little too tight but I can fit into it just still have the baby tummy and weight to lose. That's why I love this top because it lays flat in the front giving the impression of a nice stomach without the muffin top! If you gain your weight in the back I would recommend not getting this suit because it does show a lot of skin and there is a tie in the middle of back that would not be forgiving for hiding back fat.

For reference: I'm a 34D, 5' 3" and 137 lbs, squishy around the middle and pear shaped. Usually a size 10 in pants. M/L in tops and dresses. I went with a size medium. I wanted so badly to love this suit. The overall fit was great. The suit is so cute but there were a few things that I just couldn't get past. The suit is great quality for the money, I would have liked the top to be a bit more supportive but it still would have done the trick if the rest of the suit was perfect. The suit was overall pretty flattering and did a great job hiding/smoothing my torso lumps and bumps. Also, the green is gorgeous in person and the tie back is so adorable. The ugly: The leg holes are SO tight that I couldn't wait to get the suit off...I had red leg indents after 3 minutes of wear. Why do suit designers insist on making leg holes so tight? Also, the rear coverage just wasn't enough, it would have been wedgie city for me....to be fair, I do have a wide hinder so this may be perfect for small tushes.

I think I love this suit. I am a 5'6", 132 lb mom of three, and I'm just getting into one pieces at this point to have more coverage and mobility when at beach/pools with kiddos. It definitely has good tummy control, but I especially love the chest cut. I am a 32D bra size but the ladies fall quite low after 3 kids, so I don't have to worry about support and exposure. I could see this chest cut being an issue for those with more cleavage/less mom sag. I ordered a Medium and I feel like it fits perfectly. If I had any complaint it would be that it is very cheeky in the bum (photos show), and just fits my torso length (could be too short for 5'8" and up), but that is what I generally hear about Cupshe suits in general. But all in all... it's a winner in my book. We'll see how it holds up at the beach!

I absolutely love the cut of this bathing suit. The bottoms flatter my waist and hips, which I’ve always been self conscious of. It hides what I want it to hide and shows what I want it to show. However… I had to exchange this for a larger size. For reference, I am 5’5, 160 lbs and wear a bra size 36DD. I had to exchange a large for an extra large. I am normally a medium (size 8) in bottoms and large in tops, but I decided to opt for the large to accommodate for my boobs (gotta love women’s sizing). When I received the size large in the mail, the bottoms fit more like a medium, but were a bit snug on the waist. The top was a completely different story, it looked like a small and my boobs were FALLING out. The XL is better, but even so, I feel like I’m going to be adjusting it lot and accidentally showing people way more than what they wanted to see at the beach. Overall I love the cut and color of this suit, it’s super flattering and will definitely compliment my tan. However, I don’t think it’s very functional if you’re looking for a supportive top. This probably isn’t the best choice for the ladies with big tatas.

This is a re-review. (The original review is at the very bottom). Based on the chart, I originally ordered one size (XL). I am 5' 3", 145 lbs and between a 38D and a 40C. The XL turned out to be enormous on me. When they saw my initial review, the manufacturers reached out to me to offer to send me a replacement I agreed and they sent me a Large, which fits perfectly. The exercise top is well made and terrifically comfortable. The material feels very nice against the skin and I love the color. I only wish that they had put some sort of vertical reinforcements under the arms, to minimize the uni-boob/side-boob effect. All of these sorts of exercise undergarments tend to create universe, but without reinforcements on the side, if you are larger chested, you get extra bulges on the side. Aside from that, or perhaps because of that, the bra is super comfortable. Original review: I am 5'3", 145 lbs and am somewhere between a 38 and 40 C or D. Based on the chart, I ordered the XXL. (Normally, in sports bras I wear an L). I was excited to open the package, as the bra is a gorgeous color of green. But when I tried it on I was really disappointed. The bra is enormous on me, and the extra fabric hangs out. I was all the more surprised by how oversized this bra is after reading the other reviews that said the bra runs small. Not my experience. Had I realized how inaccurate their sizing chart is, I would have just ordered my regular size (L). Really disappointing. Especially given the price point, one would expect more quality control in sizing.

I thought this one piece Hot Pink AI’MAGE V Neck Tie Back Skirted bathing suit was definitely worth trying! I do not own any one piece bathing suits and only wear bikinis, bit since this bathing suit seemed to speak to me I decided why not??? The material is 82% nylon and 18% spandex. It is machine washable to 40 degrees, do not bleach, do not tumble dry. I did not wash it before trying it on so it has some wrinkles since it arrived vacuum sealed. They will disappear once washed. The size chart is accurate. I got a size Large which accommodates 38-40 bust, 30-32 waist and 38.5-40.5 hips. I am 39, 31, 38 and the size Large is perfect. I was thinking of sizing up, due to a couple of other reviews, but am very happy that I didn’t. A larger size would have been too big. I have thought of buying a one piece just to have, after all a bikini does not work on waterslides. I now have a one piece that I think looks adorable, is a great colour, great style, fits well, and I will definitely wear. The style is very flattering and you should try it!

So I loved this swimsuit when I first put it on. The fit is perfect when its dry. It has tummy control and hides my stomach. The problem is when it gets wet the white lining inside makes it sag and hang weird against the skin then that causes the material of the front of the suit to act like a suction cup to your skin. The lining used is not a good combination with the type of material of the suit (Nylon and spandex) I hate that the lining is white and shows. As you can see in my photo, I had just got out of the water and the suit is just not laying right against my skin. So if you plan to use in a public pool, you will be adjusting alot and there is nothing worse than adjusting a swimsuit over and over again. For size reference I am 5'6" 155lbs 36DD chest I am wearing the large and when it's wet there isn't much chest support. Lots of sagging and weird suction due to the nylon material. I'm sad because I can't return it now but I hope this helps someone before buying!

Summary- Versatile airy summer coverup in a shirt style that can be worn on or off the beach (vs. crochet or other sheer beachy options). Oversized just enough to feel loose and relaxed but not enormous. Materials/Construction- I usually go for cotton but this lightweight polyester feels breezy and soft. No complaints about construction. True to product photos. The sides have high slits which divides the coverup into 2 front panels and the back, which will catch a breeze nicely, keeps it from looking like just a super long button-down shirt. Fit/Sizing- I am 5'8" tall, typically a US women's size 6, fairly slender yet busty (DD). Ordered size Medium according to size chart, which is normal for online or int'l stores (in the US size 6 often aligns with Small), but I always check size charts because much of the world doesn't use the same sizing system. I'm happy with the fit, can button it up, leave it open, or even tie at my waist. Notice how long the shirt is- falls well below my rear. The high slits and open front allow more of my body to show through so it doesn't swallow me up into a shapeless blob. Personal details- I have very sensitive pale skin that burns easily, and I overheat easily too, so I use coverups in the summer over my clothes, not just my swimsuit. Sunscreen helps prevent burns but the coverup helps me feel cooler and less queasy. This will work great over a tank top and shorts. I love that the sleeves can be tabbed up or left long; I generally prefer shorter sleeves but sometimes, like when I'm driving, I want my whole arm covered.

I’m really happy with this tankini swimsuit! The tummy control feature is fantastic, offering a smooth and flattering fit without feeling too tight. The two-piece design is stylish yet provides great coverage, and I love the skirt bottom, which adds a bit more modesty while still looking chic. As for fit, I’m 5'2" and 100 lbs, and I typically wear an XS or S in tops and size 25-26 in jeans. The size S in this swimsuit fits perfectly. While I feel the top might be a bit short for someone my age, it’s not a major issue. The skirt length is just right, and the material feels comfortable and durable. The color is also exactly what I love—it’s bright but with a slightly toned-down shade, so it doesn’t stand out too much, which I appreciate. I think the swimsuit offers the perfect balance of style and comfort, and it’s great for swimming or lounging. Overall, it's a wonderful option for anyone looking for a flattering and functional swimsuit!

I rarely review items but I literally cried when I tried this swimsuit on, so it's definitely worth my time. I had a baby 5 months ago and between that, a toddler, and work, I have not been taking care of myself. I don't feel good in any clothes right now and am just really self conscious about my body. But I want to take my kids swimming this summer, so I need a suit. The pattern and color on this suit really caught my eye. First of all, the color of the swimsuit is accurate to what is shown. I was also surprised at the weight of the material; nice and thick and feels like good quality. When I looked in the mirror though I was so happy I cried. I feel so cute and a bit sexy in this swimsuit. I'm used to swimdresses looking matronly, but this one is not at all, while still modestly covering what it needs to. It's so comfortable that I want to wear it all day. For reference, I am 5'5" and 205lbs and carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I ordered an XL and while it isn't baggy, it's not tight either. It doesn't have "tummy control" or compression, but the ruching helps to conceal the mom tummy a bit. Oh, and it was important that I'd be able to breastfeed in this suit. I think that should be fairly easy given the cut of the top. I cannot recommend this swimsuit enough. When I do finally lose the baby weight, I will probably just buy another of these in a smaller size.

What my wife thinks about this swimsuit... Pros- - Super cute! I love the ruffles on the straps and the adorable flower print. Makes me think I'm younger and not wearing an old ladies swimsuit. - Comfortable. I haven't worn it in water just yet but I ran around the house in it and the straps stayed in place and it was comfortable to wear. - I like the gathered material around the waist. It wasn't tighter there (at least not on me) so I don't know if I would say it is "tummy control", but the gathered material helps to hide extra girth in that area. - I'm not a bikini bottoms type of gal, but these seem to be ok. They are high waisted and not very "cheeky"; still I may just wear my swim shorts with this top and it'll still be super cute. As a reference I am 5'2" 115 lbs and I got the M. Cons- - The only con I can see thus far is the built in bra cups. They are not sewn in and they might float around in the suit too much for my liking. I might sew them in to keep them put.

These are gorgeous swim cover-ups, as long as you do not dry them in the dryer (and probably a good idea to wash on cold/delicate cycle). However, after returning from our Cancun trip I (as always) did laundry and included this beautiful turquoise one in my colors load. I washed on cold as I always do, and dried on med-low, as I always do. Big mistake. In the pic you can see just how much it shrunk - at least 3 inches in length and probably a good 2 inches in width. These are both size L. I did not take the black one on my trip so it has never been washed. Before washing these fit very comfortably with about 2 inches of extra room around the waist when tied completely closed. Now, the turquoise one is super tight to close it completely and the length is very awkward. Also, since the length has shrunk, the “waist” now hits *right* where the underwire of my bra is (empire waist style), and it is very uncomfortable. Going to give it away to my 5’ tall niece (I am 5’8”, 177 lbs, 36DD, and wear a size 10).

I really like this cover-up. However, it was too long when it arrived. I’m guessing that is due to the XXL size. Photo 1: the coverup soon after unboxing. It was at my ankles. Photos 2-3: the coverup after I hemmed the bottom. I took it to just below my knee. I liked the arm length, but might have hemmed those too if they were longer than my elbows. The two ties in front looked like they could use some reinforcement from the sewing machine too, but so far, so good. I got the XXL due to my large bust (38DDD) and weight. I don’t like to show my legs much when wearing a swimsuit, but this coverup was a nice replacement for my usual shorts. It is light and breathable. Irons well, but wrinkles easy- That doesn’t bother me much when I’m at the beach though. Overall, I was a little bummed to have to alter it after paying for it. But, compared to other coverups that haven’t worked for my body, it was worth the extra time/effort.

This set was okay for me. I struggle with an overall size for two piece swimsuits as my bottom is usually one size larger than my top. The bottoms were snug but only provided limited compression for my curves. The top was a little on the loose side as I have a smaller bust (A cup) so did not have a flattering fit. That being said, if you are a more universal size on the top and bottom, this might be a good set for you. The quality is actually pretty nice — the garment is constructed well and it washes up nice in a cold wash with an air dry. I have not had an opportunity to wear enough yet to see if the color fades or if the fabric stretches out over time, but for now, those aspects are good. For reference, I am 5’11”, 180lbs.

I really like this bathing suit. I'm 5ft 2, 190lbs, size 38i bust and I wear about a size 17, but also fit in Walmart spandex shorts that are size 20. I usually buy size 18 in clothes. This is one of the only suits I've found that has some kind of decent support for your bust. I bounce some but not too bad. I got a size 20 to make sure the top fit and the top is slightly big, but still supportive. It's like a sports bra but it does have large, removable breast pads. I feel like the pads are adequate enough to keep in. It has a think band around the bottom of the bra that helps with support. If I order another one I'll order it in an 18 because I think the bra part will fit a little more snug which will give me a little more support. The tank top part really hides any fat rolls which is great. I feel thinner in this bathing suit. I feel really good about how it looks on me. I'm very excited to use it at the beach. The shorts fit me perfectly. I thought they would be too big but they weren't. The suit feels very comfortable as well.

So I have spent days and days going though swimsuits to find one to find my body. I'm 5'4", weigh 200lbs and carry most of it in my 40DDD's and belly. I have returned so many suits because they didn't have any kind of breast support. Yes, smaller sized women say there is support but when you have older saggy big breast, there isn't any support. I'm not going to tell you that it was excessively supportive, but it fit about like one of my old worn-out bras, which is still way more then I've found in any other suit. Since it is a bra and fits more like a bra, it works much better the others. The tank that you can wear over it covers all the rolls you want to hide. The tank is made out of the same swimsuit material and looks great. I wore it today to the lake for over 5 hours and always felt covered and comfortable with other people seeing me in it. The only downside to this suit is that the shorts fit like shorts. On my fat thighs, I have problems with shorts this length rolling up when I sit down or the seams chafing when walking. I will probably wear it with my other bottoms that are board shorts. The colors on this is so darn stinking cute and I hate to give up these shorts. I doubt I'll ever take off the tank around other people, but will at home around only my family. I'm so glad that I tried this. I never even thought about a 3 piece suit before and it is WONDERFUL! Give it a try, especially if you're large chested and having a hard time with swim suits.

3 stars about sums it up...for ref I am about 5'5, 150 lbs, 32I, size 6 pants. So pretty top heavy and generally don't wear one pieces without underwire. but hope springs eternal ;) and also I'm slightly delusional, so I grabbed this guy on Prime day in a medium and a large in different colors. PROS: the fabric is nice, the colors are pretty, the suit feels well made. the *bottom* half in the medium fit perfectly and was snug and supportive. the overall look is exactly like the photos. CONS: it's just not made for a gal with ANY b00bs. like other reviews have said--the inner cups on both the white and peach sides stick out like a sore thumb. they're woefully undersized and badly shaped, to the point of being a joke, and every stitch and piece of hardware trying to help hoist the ladies shows badly on the white side. in addition, on mine there was a piece of dark thread or something stuck between the layers on the white side of the suit. I lifted-which gave me bad side boob. I lowered-which gave me overlapping boob (by which I mean the one in the peach went over the white, not a pretty sight). I am still waiting on the large to arrive, but I have a feeling the bottom will be too big and the top will still not be big enough. Basically...just not the suit for me. I shall leave this one to my smaller chested sisters, who can remove the weird cups and look adorable.

BUY THIS SWIM SUIT! I LOVE mine. I haven’t bought a swimsuit in a decade. At my last family reunion, I went kayaking in yoga pants. I decided to take the plunge this time, and order a suit on-line. After reading reviews for days, I settled on this, and boy am I glad! PROS: I have a long torso. This suit fit very well, didn’t ride up my booty. Suit give good bust support, which I need. My shape is really weird 36D bust 30” waist 35” hips 5’7”, 120 to 125 lb Age: senior citizen/granny Suits that fit bust are usually too loose in legs. This style is very accommodating. I suspect it would be flexible for many shapes. I am older than dirt, and wanted modest coverage, but still wanted to be ‘cute’. Okay, ignore my wrinkles and pasty complexion. This suit makes me feel attractive and my DH gave it two thumbs up. The gathering top and flowing skirt give me the appearance of a waist AND, Cover my tummy and mort importantly my not so great booty. CONS I have to read up on how to set the colors, so they don’t fade in chlorine. Several people said colors ran or faded. I want to avoid that The boy shorts underneath are loose/baggy, but the skirt covers that. Narrow hips are hard to fit. I may update this review after actually wearing it in water.

I bought this suit in 2 different colors primarily for the tops. The skirted bottom is ok but I love wearing swim shorts so that’s what you see in my pictures. I’m a 38 F and the XL worked for me. The straps are adjustable so you can lift as much as you want. For comparison, I’m 5’5” and 170 lbs, normally a M-L in tops, 12-14 on bottom. I sized up to XL based on reviews. The prints on the tops do well to camouflage my low belly flab. I’m 64 years old, and I felt that the coverage was modest without being too maw-maw looking! I washed on gentle cycle with cold water and hung in laundry room to dry. It pretty much dried overnight- 12-18 hours.

This tankini has its good bits and bad bits. First the GOOD: POCKETS!! The shorts have pockets and they are cut loosely at the hem so they look more like regular shorts. The shorts have a bikini bottom built in similar to the shorts you see within a skort. The color is pretty and I like the look of a patterned top with black bottoms. The not so good: the built in bra is not supportive. The straps are extremely stretchy so they don't offer a lot of support and the built in bra is not quite long enough to cover my bust - the elastic doesn't touch my rib at all in the front. The bra's elastic band is about 1 1/2" too long, so things could slip out over time. The built in bra is also rather short so I either show a lot above or have a lot hanging out below it. Neither of which is particularly comfortable. The tiny pads drift around in the front, but stay mostly in the center where they belong. The padding should be much larger as well as the shelf bra. The seam along the top is slightly lower than the bottom of the shelf bra, but still falls a bit high. This may offer some support for smaller chested women, but given that the average American woman is a 36DD, I'm still surprised that it's extremely rare to find bathing suits built for larger busted women.

I was a little nervous ordering a swim suit online, but this fit just right. I'm 5' and weigh 140 lbs. I carry a lot of my weight in my thighs and lower belly. I have this ALLTOKE long sleeved, long pant leg swimsuit in the size large. I typically wear a size small, 4-6 top and bottom and stand 5’2” , 125#. But I usually wear a size Medium in swimsuits and biking clothes. The smallest size available in this suit when I came across it was a size large, so I got that. It fits me well everywhere except the waist and hip area. There is little extra looseness there, but not unwearable. I swim laps for an hour every day outside in the 4 or 5 months Summer season and need all the sun protection I can get. I have a long sleeved suit and some swim pants to wear with it, but all that sun protection in this one suit is why I got this. The sleeves are a little long but I was hoping for that to pull over the backs of my hands for protection there. The pant legs aren’t too long either. The body length (shoulders to crotch) seems to be just right, even though I am 5’2”. There is no looseness there. A size medium probably would have been fine for me also. There are removable bra pads and the zipper is solid. The fabric is on the thicker side, which I also like. The only thing I would change about this suit is that I would make the neck taller for more sun protection. I wear a bandana tied around my neck for more sun protection. A nice find.

I'm 5' 7" 170lbs and bought size medium. I took "un-posed" as possible pics so you can actually see what it looks like on a person just existing- not trying to look a certain way. Pros: This suit has BEAUTIFUL, vibrant colors and a lovely pattern. Flattering design- love the ruffles on the shoulders. Love that the back is a hook, not strings. Cons: The bottoms definitely don't have "tummy control" and also don't quite fit how I had hoped.. the band is kinda wide in my opinion and would be more flattering if the hip was cut higher on the leg. The bottoms are a little tight while the top is a little loose. I wish i could tighten the straps more- they came all the way tightened all the way and are too dang loose (and I'm pretty normal when I comes to sizing usually). The bra pads are almost too small for the bikini and they're kinda flimsy. Summary: This bikini is super adorable, good for the price, decently comfortable, and does the job. Don't expect anything super fancy, but this is great for just a normal- ass bikini to wear to the pool or whatever. You probably won't feel like a sexy goddess, but seriously, it's summer. Don't worry too much about how you look, just go have fun 💓

This is a very cute suit. It feels like a more modest option which I appreciate on days when I feel like hiding my post-partum tummy a bit. It does a good job of camoflauging parts I might not feel like highlighting most days. This suit is truly adorable while being attractive and still age-appropriate for us grown folks (Please don't take this the wrong way but int he spirit is is intended - By this I mean not looking like a larger version of a toddler's one-piece, NOT that we have to tone it down and hide or some such nonsense as we get older). I love the little skirt. It is sewn onto the body and is not removable. This is what I expected from the ad but I wish it was only sewn onto the hips so that I had the option to tighten or loosen the skirt by tying it like a wrap-around dress. This is a super small complaint, though. Overall this is beautiful and flattering.The skirt hits just under the swell of my hip, not too cheeky, just right, super flattering. I'm 5'4" for reference and the lowest part of the front of the skirt hits 4" under my bellybutton. I am 160lbs, 38D/ 43" bust and 34.5" waist and 44.5" hips and I picked the size XL and it fits very nicely, a bit loose but supportive enough. The bottoms under the skirt are connected tot he one-piece as well of course and have high french-cut leg openings. The skirt covers most of this, like 905 of my bum in these. It shows just enough to be flirty without having my cheeks out which is cool for some days, like for example my nieces swim party where I don't want to wrestle with a cover-up all day and not want to take it off even though I feel like swimming because I would feel a tiny bit self-concious in one of my bikinis around my extended family and their kids (It shouldn't really matter and it's not a big deal I just mean it's nice to have more covered options on some occasions, you know?.). This suit is a vibrant color just as pictured. A beautiful rose red. It is lined and is comfortable. It is true to size. i did measure it and it matches the provided chart. It has removable very flat round nipple-cover-type pads in the bust area. You can simply pull them out (or add larger ones) if you want. I like the nice thick supportive straps and the metal hardware that will last without chipping and breaking like plastic can. I like that it isn't overly padded too. It has no underwire. The fabric is thick and feels like quality material.There were a few small areas that had loose stitching on the shoulder ties but I usedmy sewing machine to fix these. They were tiny and I didn't mind fixing them but I wanted to mention them because no one else should have to sew a brand-new suit. This may be something that rarely happens with its company's products, Idk, but that is why I took off a star.

In order to get the straps over my head to do the crisscross in the back, I have to loosen the straps up all the way. Well, once the straps are on, then it is quite difficult to adjust them back. I need an extra set of hands with this suit. Let’s just say, it would be awful if I was at a public pool in this suit and had to use the bathroom. What a hassle. Other than those issues, everything else was fine. The fit is true. I’m 5’6, 150#, 34D, and normally a size medium in one-piece swimsuits. I ordered the medium in this suit and it fits, I might have also fit a large. The size chart was pretty wishy-washy about the bust size and cup size. My size fell into two different sizes, so I just went with what I know. Overall, a cute swimsuit but just not what I expected in more ways than one.

I do not often write a review, but had to share how great this coverup is! It is soft, lightweight and feels great against my skin (makes we want to sleep in it!). I am tall and have long legs, and coverups often barely cover everything. This coverup is long enough to fully cover my backside. I will be comfortable going from beach/pool to a restaurant at a resort wearing this. The sleeves are loose and flowy, very comfortable.I machine washed it using a lingerie bag and hung it to dry. The tag said NOT to machine dry it. Fabric is rayon, which should never be dried (which is why some reviewers experienced major shrinkage I think). Being rayon, it dried with wrinkles. I ordered the blue tye dye, so they are not really noticeable. If wearing as a dress I would iron it. I think the wrinkles would definitely be more noticeable on the solid fabrics.I am 5'9." My measurements are 43" (DD), 40", 47." According to size chart I could have ordered XXL, but after reading reviews, I decided to order the XL, and I am 100% pleased with the fit. If you do not mind line drying your coverup, I highly recommend this item!

I ordered a couple of sizes and one other swim dress from a different brand/ seller, but this is the CLEAR winner! 1. It makes me feel GREAT! SO cute! 2. It's NOT A ONE-PIECE! I hate wearing one-piece swimsuits because logistically trying to get them off, and especially back on (ahem, restroom visit), while they are wet is literally the worst. This is essentially a dress tankini , so whatever bottoms you like best, you can wear those! I have a high waisted set from Torrid that I love, and I'll be wearing those with it most likely. 2. Length is that just-right-still-short-enough-to-be-flirty sundress vibe! 3. Shape - bodice crossover design and waist yoke with ruching will make you look like an hourglass! 4. Straps are wide - super silky and comfy, and they don't take bites out of your shoulders - PLUS, better support! 5. Color is true to the photo - I love purple, and this is a nice blend of a deep violet, lilac, periwinkle, and lavender with some black, grey, and white in a floral that doesn't look dated. 6. Skirt has plenty of ease! I bought the 16 and 18 (I'm 5'56", ~200lb, and I'm at least one size larger from the waist down). I wound up going with the 16, because the skirt had plenty of ease to accommodate my larger hips, and the 18 was not as supportive in the bust. OVERALL, well made, comfy, and a delight to wear. I didn't want to take it off, so I've been wearing it around the house ALL day. If you've been thinking about it, just order one and send it back if you don't like it!

I prepared for it not to fit, etc. and to be disappointed before I tried it on. The cups are moveable but seemed to stay in place, and my boobs have no volume or lift after years of nursing my kids. But if I moved the cups down, they actually held them in place, which normally swimsuits never do. The cutout is in a good place and the bottom area actually has perfect coverage. Not so much that you feel like a grandma, but enough that you’re not scared your cheeks are going to show when you bend over. I gave it 4 stars only because I wish the bottom part had some tummy control to it, but it doesn’t. I am 5’4” and currently 152 lbs. and after my c-section with my now-toddler, I can’t even successfully suck in my stomach anymore. I wish the mom pooch was held back some. Overall though, this is the best swimsuit I’ve tried on in probably a decade. I always go for black due to weight, but this is so fun and the colors are beautiful in person and exactly as in the pics. My camera doesn’t do the colors justice. It seems to be well-made and I feel good after trying it on. Not happy with my weight, but this swim suit at least doesn’t make me dread summer as much. And now I can have some color to my swimwear! I read the reviews and there were so many great ones with so many different body types, I hoped it would be flattering for me too and I think it is, as much as it can be. Highly recommend!

I’m a longtime customer of quality swimsuits, many from J. Crew. But I decided to take a chance on this inexpensive swimsuit and I am thoroughly satisfied. I am a middle-aged, 5-foot-8-inch woman (and mom!), a size 16, with a D to DD cup size bra. This swimsuit fits perfectly. Also, the material is strong and keeps any wobbly bits looking smooth and firm. I especially love the tummy control lining, which is covered with shirred material for a flattering look. The bra and shoulder straps are also supportive if you are busty. I know how difficult it is for plus-size women to find flattering swimsuits that they can swim well in, without a lot of water drag due to excess material, as well as feel confident in. I hope my review and photo will help other plus-size women try this suit, because I know many of you want to be active at the beach, lake or pool, as I always am, and we should feel just as stylish as thinner women when enjoying physical activities — and life!!! Many of my mom friends are thinner than me and younger than me, but I am every bit as active as them. So when I hang out with them at the pool, I think I should feel every bit as stylish and confident as them, despite my size — and you should, too! Don’t cover up with a bulky swimsuit — get out there and enjoy your body in this flattering swimsuit, you amazing curvy ladies!

I ordered this top to wear as multi-purpose travel clothing, for the occasional swim or beach visit, but also to have in my warm weather appropriate wardrobe to wear as a tunic length sleeveless top.For reference, I have a full bust measurement of 44 inches, I wear a 34F bra but have am a size 8 from the waist down. According to the size chart with my bust measurement my best fit was XXL, but I knew that wouldn’t fit. The XL shelf bra is a bit short and offers no support, but it does include modesty pads (they are NOT support cups) for nipple coverage which is a nice benefit in a swim top. I’ll have to add length to the shelf bra and likely will also add 2” elastic banding to the bottom in lieu of the current 1” elastic. The entire front of the swim suit has at least 2 layers of fabric, the back has only one except for the band for the shelf bra. I will only wear this top over a wired bra or swim bra for support.The XL skims my torso and flares out a bit in the last 2 or so inches before the hem, which hits me at the low hip. The ruching is cute both for wearing as a swim top and also when I wear it as a sleeveless tunic, but it makes the front of the top droop lower than the back, despite my bust size.There isn’t anything that would be considered shaping or “tummy control” fabric or construction in the suit or liner. The lining is made entirely of lightweight quick dry stretch fabric so it feels nice and soft against the skin and I imagine it will perform well as swimwear because the lining doesn’t hold water. The outer fabric is quite a nice weight, I’d call it mid-weight nylon spandex and it is considerably heavier fabric than is often used for swimwear. This is a benefit because it will be less clingy due to the weight of the fabric. There is no additional banding or compression fabric between the outside fabric and the liner fabric, so I am not sure why this is called tummy control.

The “BMJL Women's Swimsuit Coverup Swim Beach Cover up Swimwear Lace Bathing Suit Cover ups Dresses” arrived promptly and was lovelier than described online. The beautiful white tiered fabric with intricate bodice lacework really stands out when viewed up close. All eyes are sure to turn in your direction when you set foot on the sand or appear at the pool wearing this airy and lightweight beach coverup! I ordered size “XL” for my 5’8” height, 160# and 38B. The strikingly feminine coverup is 34” in length when measured from the back neck down to the hem. The sleeves are 18-1/2” in length, falling with one gentle ruffle to below the elbow. Three tiers of gently gathered ruffles cascade under the bodice. There is a 6-1/2” neck opening in the front bodice for over-the-head easy on / easy off. A lovely 22” tassel tie offers optional closure at the round neckline. Excellent materials and superb craftsmanship are found throughout this loose-fitting, cool and airy garment. Fabric is 95% Polyester for durability and 5% Spandex for very slight elasticity. All seams are encased, with no loose threads. The high quality lace insert is strong and sturdy, yet remains delicate in appearance. There is great attention to detail wherever you look. This is obviously a garment that was made with high standards and pride. As a designer and seamstress of bridal gowns, I am very impressed with the excellent quality of this cover-up. Like all fine clothing, hand washing and air drying is recommended. I might fluff this garment in the dryer on “air only” for a minute or two as well and immediately hang to dry. This well-made garment is an excellent value for the money. I believe you will be very pleased, as I am, with your choice of adding this lovely BMJL cover-up to your resort / cruise-wear, summer or vacation wardrobe. Enjoy! Highly Recommended :)

I ordered another color of this suit back in May 2022 in the Aqua Navy printed. I mainly got it because I have a new grandbaby and wanted a suit that I didn't have to keep adjusting while trying to wrangle a newborn. The suit is well made, thick, nice quality, and lined. Score! I did not have the riding up issues that others mentioned and I actually liked the sewn in bra because again I didn't want something that had to be readjusted all the time. The sewn in bra was a rectangle shape like a traditional bra shape. The first one I bought was a size large. I am 5'3 and 128lbs and it fit fine, was not tight but I knew it would and it did get looser when wet. I bought another one because the first one worked out so well but ordered a medium this time to account for the looser when wet fit. It arrived today and it is still well made, thick, nice quality, and lined BUT there is a change. The padding for the bra is no longer sown in and can be removed. So it is no longer "sewn-in soft bra padding and elastic chest band provide more support" as stated in the description or like the one I bought in May 2022. Which also means it will constantly be moving around when you are moving around, swimming or playing with little ones and have to be adjusted repeatedly. As soon as I tried it on I had to adjust the padding in the bra which are 2 circle shapes, not triangular. They are really more to cover any area that you wouldn't want poking out, not for support, which detracts from the whole purpose of having a sewn in bra. Now it is only a built in padding not a built in bra which is something that I like and something I didn't have to constantly keep adjusting when wet or moving or fix after it comes out of the wash. I still give it 5 stars for quality, fits well and the suits being well made I just wish they had left the bra part alone.

Like many other reviewers I've been looking for a swimsuit that I feel cute in but isn't too revealing- when at the beach with my two young kids I don't want to feel too exposed. This fits the bill and seems well-made, though I haven't gotten it wet yet. I appreciated other reviewers posting pictures and sharing their size so thought I'd do the same. I'm a little under 5'7" and I'm 125 pounds, and pretty flat-chested. Many reviews had me thinking I'd be in the size small camp, but I also have a long torso and some recommended sizing up for that. And I was undecided on colors, so I got a medium in blue floral and a small in yellow to try on. Overall the difference in size is minimal. The small is a little more clingy, but the medium doesn't feel like it's going to fall off by any means. The biggest difference is in the seat of the swimsuit. Neither one feel like they're riding up or choking off my, er, lady parts, even with the extra layer of underwear for trying on. That's a miracle for someone with a long torso. But the small did seem to be squishing into my butt cheeks a little more. It's not that it's too short length-wise, but more like narrower from front to back, without much give. It wasn't so much as to be uncomfortable, but it was more noticeable visually- it was creating a little valley at the edge of the swimsuit into my butt cheek, if that makes sense. That made me a little more self-conscious, plus I find the blue floral color and pattern a little more flattering overall, so I'm sticking with a medium. If your body has a little more up top than below a small might be fine, though the bust area in both felt pretty good- I didn't feel like there was some huge gap between my actual chest and the fabric of the swimsuit, which is also nice. If someone's bigger on top the lacing in the back could be loosened up to provide more room, but the seat of the swimsuit just is what it is, so rounder bottoms might want to aim for a medium. :)

Like many women, i HATE bathing suit season but love being by the water. I gave birth to my son a year ago and have been DREADING shopping for new swimwear, especially after being through the pandemic and putting a few extra pounds on!! I found this swimsuit while looking for tankinis, and was in love with how unique and chic it looked on everyone in the reviews. Ladies, JUST BUY IT ALREADY! You could pay $150 for a comparable one at Nordstrom, or spend $30 and have more money to spend on accessories!!! It is sooooo flattering on my new mom-bod. This highlights all the right places and disguises the bad ones! The thought of a sheer middle made me uneasy BUT it actually creates a pretty awesome optical illusion and makes your waist look smaller! The material is super thick and soft. For reference, I am short and curvy: 5’0”, 155lbs, 36D and got the black in 10-12. Fits like a glove and the straps aren’t too big or loose. Also has great butt coverage and leg openings. It washes great if done on a cool/gentle setting, then hung dry. Because of the mesh pieces I want to be careful not to warp them in the dryer. My only issue is that the criss-cross on top is pretty big and makes a funny tan line! You can lower the straps and push them off your shoulders for a sleeveless suit. Hope this helps! 😉

I also think the white looks a little dingy now although I washed with white clothes only. I recommend hand washing just to be safe. I've never order a swim suit online before, so I ordered three suits from different brands and this is the one I am keeping. For starters, the quality of the material is top notch, the colors are vibrant, and the entire suit is lined. The material is thick but stretches nicely. I noticed there were some complaints about the torso of this suit being too short, but that was not an issue for me. I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs., and I ordered a large. The only area of the suit that didn't fit perfectly was the chest area, but I'm a B cup so I figured a size large might be too big in the chest. However, it wasn't too big to the point it looks bad or feel uncomfortable. Everything feels "tuck in" and the suit has nice bottom coverage. It's a great suit for moms that don't want to be falling out everywhere when playing in pool or on the beach with their kids. The style is super flattering and my kids told me I looked pretty the first time I wore it. I highly recommend this suit!!

I have a toddler so if I'm at beach or pool I'm up and down, running around like a chicken with its head cut off. I'm a lady who wants every inch of cheek fully covered without having to constantly pull it down. I'm 5'2 135-140lbs post baby and got a 10 (6 was recommended). If I hadn't sized up, the torso would not have been long enough- and really isn't even now. I'd love the band to hit just below my boobs but it is a couple inches below...I can only imagine how low it would be on a smaller size. I obtained the full cheek coverage I desired, at the cost of the top cups being too large. I'm a 32c and breastfeeding so my boobs are heavy and I wish it had more support. Yet it looks beautiful on and I can nurse in it, and the butt stays in place. So I'm still on the fence whether to keep it. In bikinis I get a small, tight top for support and a larger bottom for coverage. It's hard with a one piece to choose which area to sacrifice...an uncomfortable choice no matter what. Last summer I had support but had to wear short to cover my rear. Now I'll have rear coverage but the pain of saggy boobs whipping around. Ugh IDK.

I LOVE my blue one of these. It has a Hilor label, and is size 10. (Even though I'm a 4-6 in real life, 5'8, 140.) It is beautiful and covers me well but still feels pretty. So I ordered a black one which was sent with NO tags, no Hilor label, and said it's a size L (not even an option - I ordered another 10!). Most bizarrely, it was sent from an unnamed sender in Kansas City. It fits terribly, has white cups inside (which don't blend in with the black), and is poorly sewn. Just reordered and this one is beautiful - cups are black, fabric is a richer black, ruching is more pronounced (and hides belly better), and stitching and macrame are better and stronger. Even the boob cups overlap more/provide better hold and coverage at front. Definitely return if you don't get one with a Hilor tag and a numerical size - these other ones are awful!

I’ve put on weight between having my daughter & recently even a bit more from stress. That being said, I wanted a bathing suit to cover and camouflage the damage (weight gain), remaining covered while chasing & swimming with my toddler, plus look/feel good in it & hopefully even sexy. This mostly fit the bill, while the strappy back design seems cute, it’s not the easiest to get properly into place & can make back fat a little bit noticeable, the lower back pinched in a little as well. Think I’m possibly returning it, as the back straps being a pain to manage to get right & not entirely flattering as well as the lower back area pinching my fat a bit too. For reference I’m 5’2, about 151-152lbs. 38DD. Update: I’ve been working out & watching what I eat (for about 4 weeks now) & have lost a few lbs. & at least a inch in my waist. I feel like the back of the bathing suit is already fitting better/less pinching of my fat & is overall more flattering now. Still wish the back straps didn’t require so much fooling around with to make it look as is intended. I added updated pics for comparisons sake, it should be the last 3pics.

I am 42 years old, 5’9” and about 160lbs with a ‘mom bod’ (extra pooch in the gut area Thanks to aging, hormones, and two kids). I am wearing a size 10 in these pictures. I normally wear a size 6 or 8 in jeans, and a medium or large in t shirts, a size 7 in underwear and 34-36B in a bra. This suit is very snug on me, and I could have probably gotten away with a size 12 for comfort. The cups are very well padded and even make the girls look nice. The padding is the type that is meant to shape, and won’t bunch up when washed. The ruching in the front is very flattering and sort of hides any of your own imperfections. There is full butt coverage, and the straps are adjustable. This is my first one one-piece since my hot 20 year old bod left me years ago. I wasn’t a one-piece gal. As long as you can wear a bikini, that means the aging process stops, right? bikini or die! Well, that’s not correct. I got older, my body got mommy-er, and I wasn’t ready for the one-piece. When I finally accepted that I could not reverse time, I went looking for a one-piece, and a Dodge Caravan. I found this suit, and I LOVE it. One of the best bathing suits, bikini or not, I’ve ever purchased.

As a middle aged lady who, like many of us, is carrying a few more pounds than she would prefer, I’m a little self conscious about wearing a bathing suit. I really want to be able to enjoy the beach and pool, though, so I’ve been searching for a new suit that would give me coverage I like and that I will feel comfortable wearing in public - and this one hits the mark! I’ll describe what I like, from top to bottom: The wider straps help provide more support and don’t cut into my shoulders. The higher back coverage makes my whole torso look a little slimmer and feels supportive (holds in a couple lumps and bumps). The chest has good support and isn’t low cut. The ladies are able to stay in place without being smushed together or bulging out the top. The ruching across the mid section camouflages my stomach. The print also provides camouflage (I don’t think I would like this suit in a solid color). The skirt skims over my hips. It’s not clingy at all, so it also covers my less than toned thighs. The “shorts” are wonderful under the skirt, as they truly do give upper thigh support. Overall, I think this a GREAT suit, and the price is on point. For reference, I’m between 5’4” and 5’5” and currently weigh about 188. Happy Shopping!

First, just so this is clear first because it is hard to tell from the listing if someone doesn't read the description carefully: this is only for the TOP. There is no bottom (shorts). I bought this for my wife so this is what she had to say about it: "I usually wear a Size 14 (on the bottom because I am curvy) and an X or XL on the top. My breast size is 36B/36C, so quite the little breasts. I ordered the top a 14Plus. I had a hard time putting this over my head. Just under the breast the material is quite tight around my body and very weirdly cut (maybe look at the back of the top), way too thin where the straps stop and then the rest of the top falls over the back. I bought a similar top and it is way longer - in fact it just covers my butt. This top is too short for this, so you can see my cheeks. Eww. The straps in back have a clasp that can slide out of the fabric (during swimming for example) - see the close up photo. Not cool! My other swimsuit has a closed clamp, not an open one. The worst part is that there is no hem at the bottom of the top - it's just cut. Someone wanted to save in the manufacturing process by skipping a step! This makes this top look really cheap. The good part is, it does somewhat disguise one's tummy. But it doesn't disguise one's bottom. The fabric is cute but it is hard to find a color-coordinated bottom that would fit with this busy pattern. As you can see from the photos, I am not a heavy person - some even wonder why I am a 14 (well, because of my curvy bottom....). So I am very surprised that this 14Plus top is super tight on me. I do not think I will wear it at all.... it feels way too small, too short, too cheap and if that clasp slips away from the fabric, how can I reach back there to fix it?! I won't be able to. Too risky, especially while swimming. This top had potential, but..... Oh, and the price is quite high for the fact that you get only the top, but not a matching bottom.

I'm doing this review because I lean heavily on reviews with pictures of women who look like me and have similar shape. For reference, I weigh 219, I'm 5'8", bust 41, waist 40, hips 50. I hope this review helps a fellow plus size gal!

For starters - I CANNOT believe I'm posting these pics of me in this bathing suit. But I always wish people my size would, to help me determine what size I need! I'm 5'3, go between 200-209lbs. About 205lbs in this Pic. I wear a 14 or 16 in most jeans (Old Navy, American Eagle). I wear a Large in most active shorts and Large or XL tee depending if I want a good fit or over size look. This bathing suit is a 14/16. I personally have a large belly (not rolls but literally looks pregnant 🤦🏻‍♀️). So I prefer a looser top that isn't quite as tight as this one. If you have a normal belly this would 100% be true to size!!! I really do NOT like the bottoms. They fit very weird, and will NOT stay up. I would spend my entire day holding them up if I wore them. I definitely wouldn't wear them to a water park or anywhere with a slide. They WILL fall off. They like bunch up a bit in the front (almost looks like you have a huge bush in your public area pushing it out 🤦🏻‍♀️). It does have adjustable straps which I LOVE. It provides great support and holds your boob's up nicely 😂 It would be very comfortable if it weren't for my stomach which shouldn't be an issue for the average person - other than the bottoms. Sadly I will have to return because I just won't be comfortable with it as tight as it is on my stomach and it had a small tear in the side as pictured. I actually wouldn't even call it a tear - it just looks like I happened to get a bad one that wasn't finished sewing. I'm not a super picky person so I'd say it's a great suit for the money (if I had ordered the correct size to give my stomach more room). It's a good quality fabric that feels great! Not that cheaper see thru quality like alot of stuff I've gotten on amazon. It's a super cute style and I think I will absolutely love the larger size!!!

I'm 5'6", 200 lbs, and something like a 36DD. This bikini top fits me quite nicely and provides a little more coverage than typical slinky tops, so I feel like it's safer for around family, or the neighbors, etc. Also, I appreciate that it's not yet another halter style top so my neck isn't doing all the work to hold up the weight of my ta-tas. It does have an underwire, which I usually avoid, but this wasn't at all uncomfortable. And the padding is minimal, which is more my style. This is NOT any sort of push up top, which is fine by me since I'm not interested in an inch of extra padding, but just something to note. The extra fabric below the normal bra band is something to be aware of since if you're more curvy like me that could potentially end up riding up. But so far, it's a cute top and I'm liking it. I might even also play around with re-stringing the back ties so that the straps cross in the back and then connect to the opposite side since I think that will provide some better security with holding the shoulder straps up so that they aren't sliding down my shoulders if I'm in the ocean battling waves. Ultimate result: I'm happy and really like it!

I bought this suit on a whim, after reading an article on social media, of how much this item was selling on Amazon. I have Prime but, the suit was sent via USPS and it was delayed by 1 day, which was very inconvenient, as I wanted to wear it to the beach, but, oh well. Anyway, when I finally got it and put it on...WOZA! absolute perfection! my husband said I looked 20 pounds thinner, it is soooo flattering! oh my goodness! the fabric is the right thickness, and just holds everything in without discomfort. I will buy it in more colors, it is gorgeous. I can't vouch for longevity, or how it will be after wet, but the hundreds of positive reviews say it all.

First off, I must confess I had low expectations when I ordered this tankini top, as I generally have terrible luck with swimsuits that turn out to be too tight or ill-fitting. This tankini was a pleasant surprise. I'm 5'3", weigh about 175-180 pounds and usually wear a 40C bra. I ordered it in a size 16 and was thinking maybe I should've ordered it in a size 18, afraid it would end up being too small (especially after reading some of the reviews), as so many swimsuits are not really sized anywhere near correctly and are frequently too tight. The sweetheart-shaped neckline and the look of the gathered ruching and the surplice wrap at the bustline are very flattering. All that said, swimsuits are almost always hit-or-miss and a very personal (and often painful) affair for most women, and we always take our chances when ordering on line, but I was lucky with this one. It fit true to my size. I think I might just order another one in black!

I never feel comfortable in swimsuits. They are never flattering and always either make me feel like a stuffed sausage, a granny, or like my girls will pop out at any moment. I'm mid weight-loss and didn't want to spend a ton of money on a new suit for vacation where it would only likely be used once, but needed something so bought a few and hoped for one that at least fit, even if it wasn't flattering. I'm 5'4", about 200lbs, and hold the majority of my weight in my stomach. Typically wear a 2xl top and 16 bottom. Ended up buying size 18. Well, this is the first swimsuit I've worn in probably 20 years that I actually feel comfortable in. It's not just plain black or covered in flowers, it isn't putting my rolls on display, but it's also not totally conservative either. The girls aren't in danger of flopping out, but the mesh slit in the front is a nice peek-a-boo. The ruching on the lower half helps hide my stomach, while the two tone color draws attention up and away from my stomach as well. I feel like it flatters me pretty well. The top is ever so slightly too big for me, but if I went down a size the bottom half would be too tight. For the price, it also feels pretty well made. I wish it had slightly better support for the girls, but at least they don't droop out to each side and look that way like so many other suits. Definitely recommend this swimsuit, especially if you're apple shaped and struggle to find things like me.

I ordered this in a 2XL or 18W and it fit perfect. I am holding a lot of my whopping 68lb pregnancy weight so I don’t know my exact weight. I’m currently in either a 16 or 18 in jeans. I’m at least 210lb and I have a D-DD sized cups for reference. Overa definitely recommend and aqua Eve has other suits that are also awesome! You won’t be disappointed.

So flattering and incredibly comfortable! Even supportive for my 38DDD (G) chest! I've never worn a 2-piece before, and I've certainly never put bathing suit pictures of myself onto any website/app. But being a thick woman, I want to give an honest review and honest pictures to help other thick women! I'm 5'2", 250, apple-shaped, and normally wear an 18 or 20. I initially ordered a 20 in this suit because of my chest size, but decided it was too large and then sent it back and ordered an 18 (returns are SUPER easy BTW). The 18 fits terrific!!! I do wonder if a 16 would be even more supportive for my chest though, so might order one and then update this post. I love the truly high-waisted bottoms as well as the adjustable straps on the top... as a short woman, I have a short length from my shoulders to my chest and always need to shorten straps as far as possible, and this top allows for that! The straps are also removable, in the fact that you can make them cross in the back or just go straight down. In the first picture, the straps are crossed in the back, the second picture they're going straight down - crossing them shortens the straps a little and adds more support for us large-chested ladies. All in all, I'm SUPER HAPPY with this purchase and how confident it makes me feel! And for only $40 this is a steal!!! UPDATE: I've worn it roughly 20 times, and it fits just like the first wear - holds up well, no stretching!

Update: Still LOVE this suit! It's 7+ months later, and a full summer of boating & swimming has gone by. And the suit held up well, and still fits well, even as I'velost 110+ lbs. Edges are still fine. Colors are still bright. Suit is extremely comfortable to wear all day on the water. I'll be ordering a new one soon in a different style for next summer season. Just came in today. Super cute & fits great! It's the 5th suit I've bought this season, and first one I'll be able to keep. Yeah! I'm 5ft 2 in, & weigh 203#. I wear size 16 regulars & XL in clothes, but always buy a size 18 bathing suit. After returning 3 others (and not able to fit comfortably in the 4th suit after washing), I decided to try this 18W. My measurements are 46 bust, 43 waist, 46 hips, so I was worried the waist might be a bit snug since the sizing chart says it's a 39.5 inch waist, and I would need a 20W for me. But my bust & hips were middle of the range for the 18W. The flowing A-style design flows down from just under the bust & flows over the waist & belly. Shorts are well-fitting too, with plenty of coverage all around & not tight at all. The back is traditional straight line, not too high for me. Straps are medium width, and the girls are well-supported. It's a printed design (on one side only), printed onto white material. Colors are vibrant. The edge is NOT hemmed / sewed. I don't like that, but have struggled to find anything with a sewn hem. So decided to give this try after reading thousands of reviews. I may take it to my local tailor to see if she'll sew it for me. Until then, I'll see how well it holds up in the wash.

I bought this suit to swim laps at my local pool and was extremely happy to see it has a CLOSED back with no opening at the middle of my back! I originally bought this suit and another boy short model that had that stupid mid back opening and returned it immediately because it was so uncomfortable- I could tell it definitely would have been an issue in the pool. Also, the other brand’s suit (that was new!) came dirty with white lines all over the black suit (maybe deodorant??) gross. This suit however came clean and fits GREAT because the back is fully covered except for at your shoulders with the slight criss cross material which is nice to add a bit of fun to the suit. It doesn’t inhibit my swimming at all while working out doing freestyle laps. I’ve worn this suit 3 times now since I got it this weekend and have hand washed it just as much—the color has held up beautifully. The tummy control feature is very subtle (meaning it’s not like spandex and it won’t feel tight while working out in the water, but it’s definitely there)! The straps are sturdy and the bikini fit at the bottom is very comfortable. I’m only 5 feet tall and 200 pounds, so a well fitting suit is VERY hard to find, especially one to workout in, but I’m extremely happy with this purchase! The stretch is perfect and the chest support is great- I never ever have to adjust it when I’m in the water and I don’t feel that it gets looser once I’m in the water, either. I also want to add that taking the cups out was easy and the suit isn’t too see through without them, either, which is important for such a bright color screaming “look at me”! Sorry for not including a pic of me actually in the suit, but I figured I should show the beautiful vibrant pink color is true to the listing photos! Very happy!!! 10/10 and will definitely be getting more for my swim workouts! :) thank you!!
