Fit perfectly. I'm 5'1" 185lbs, 40DD and a total apple shape. I can never find swimsuits that don't squish, or make me feel like a sausage. This is perfect. It covers what I want covered, with graceful and flowing fabric in the front, so it doesn't feel like a mumu. I have a very small bottom, and it still fits perfectly. Comes down on my legs so nothing is exposed, but again, I'm not wearing a tent. Bust is supported, as well as any suit does, plenty of room, but not too much. I don't feel like anything is going to pop out, and it fits nicely on the shoulders. It doesn't make me look like a size 10, but I'm not ashamed to get in the pool. Quality of fabric and stitching both seem good. All in all, a good purchase. I'm quite happy with this!

I haven't bought a two piece swimsuit in years. I decided to step outside my comfort zone and bought this and I am so happy that I did! I love it! I bought the size 18 but I honestly could have gotten a size 16. I may order another in a smaller size to wear on my "skinny" days. I love this suit. Very cute. I get lots of compliments. The green is very vibrant and a true emerald. The bottoms are very forgiving. They hide a multitude of sins. Lol. The cut is very low on the legs, and there is plenty of coverage for your behind. Plus, the rouching camouflages that muffin top very well. I'm 5'5", and the waist can be pulled up almost to my chest if I want it to. Or you can slouch it down a little if you don't have a many rolls to conceal! And the top is great except for one thing. The wider straps are great, and you can even move them. They have hooks so you can uncross them if you prefer. The cup size is generous and has removable pads. However...You can't adjust the width of the band at all. My chest is only a 38, even though I wear a 16 in jeans. So the 18W fit perfectly on the bottom, and the cup size of the top fit my DDD chest just right, but the band is a little loose which makes the girls sag a little because I can't tighten the straps too much without the band riding up. Other than that, great suit! I may try taking in the sides a little if I get brave!

I appreciate this brand because you can get the larger sizes not just up to a XL or XXL. That said I wish you could buy the top and bottom separately. I'm 5'7" 209 pounds and a 42DD. I usually have to purchase them on a site that is for plus size so it's nice to find them on amazon prime. I bought this for the top, I went with a 18plus which should have been perfect but as you can see I do have side boob going on even with the triangles pulled apart. It doesn't bother me but if it does you keep that in mind. I think it mostly fits the next size up might have been better but I was in a hurry for my Maui trip. The bottoms were huge which I knew they would be. Luckily I found the same Tempt Me brand in the same color (which I have on in the pics) a little different style which I actually preferred to this sets original style bottom and in an XL which fit perfect. The color was perfect since it was the same brand. Sucks you have to buy two suits but its sorta the way life is in the plus size world. The only negative I would say as the suit is pretty well made is the color bleeds really bad. I soaked it in vinegar all day before I left changing the water out multiple times. Then washed it on delicate cycle and hung to dry. It bled on the towel I was sitting on both days I wore it, also stained the white tank top arm pits I wore over it. Still trying to figure out how to get it to stop bleeding. But I do really like the suit and the color is very vibrant.

This bathing suit is super cute! I am normally an 18W, 2X, 42D bra. I have been on a weight loss journey, so I got my normal size of 18W, instead of sizing up to a 20W. The 18W fit perfectly! I was expecting it to be too tight because I normally have to size up in swimsuits, so I was pleasantly surprised. I could lose another 10lbs and it would still look great too. I don't have the confidence for a bikini, so the cutout lets me show a little skin without being a full out bikini. The tie in the center of the bust allows for some contour and makes the boobs look great. I am on the smaller chest size, so I think this swimsuit has great support and coverage. I'm not sure how it would be for someone with larger breasts. The clasp in the back allows for adjustment across the chest area so you can customize your fit a bit. The straps and attachments are all sewn well and look like they will be very durable. The inner part of the swimsuit is a mesh that really helps to sculpt and hold things in. I instantly felt thinner in this suit because it helped to smooth out my lumps, bumps, jiggles, and rolls. The color contrast on the top compared to the bottom helps to draw the eye upward, which is a benefit for me because my hips are my widest and biggest part. The back comes up high enough to provide good coverage and hid all of my back fat rolls. The legs are not high cut and i found that they provided great coverage and didn't let any hip or lower belly fat show. I think I have a normal length torso, so if you have a long torso, it might not be the same for you. I washed it on cold and hung to dry and it didn't fade or shrink at all. I'm looking forward to wearing this through the summer!

I’m a large lady. This doesn’t hide that even a little, but I don’t care. The quality and color is fantastic and I feel hot in it! There isn’t enough rouche to cover the stomach and the top doesn’t come down to cover my back rolls, but I don’t personally are about people seeing those anyway. (My personal philosophy is if I can’t see it, I don’t care what it looks like; and very rarely do I catch a glimpse of my back.) If that’s important to you, keep that in mind. Regardless, you’ll look absolutely killer in this suit. I typically wear a size 18-20 in the women’s plus size section. (18 in stretchier stuff, 20 in less forgiving materials.) I typically like my bathing suits snugger and based on the reviews, I went with the 18 plus thinking it would be a little small. It really wasn’t. The bottoms slipped on perfectly. They’re not baggy or ready to fall off or even slip down, but they’re not quite snug either. I’d call them true to size if nothing else. I’d be willing to try the 16 bottoms, but the top would be too small for my chest. They don’t come separately as far as I can tell so the 18 it is! (Which is honestly fine, it’s still comfortable.) My bra is size 44DDD/44F and this top was snug. I could maybe stand to go up a size, but then the bottoms would be too big. It’s not uncomfortable to wear this size. I definitely couldn’t go smaller. It does compress my chest down a little bit, but that’s mostly because I had it tight enough to keep my breasts from moving ANYWHERE. It’s easy to tighten and loosen the straps, and I might loosen them a little next time I try it on. Either way, this is an incredibly supportive top (especially for being a wireless bikini) as a larger breasted woman. I love it. And the style makes them look *chef’s kiss*. Overall, I’m a big fan and ready to rock my vacation next week. I’m going to be a fat babe on the beach and I’m looking forward to it. Let them stare, babes. This beach body is READY! 😎

I’m 4’11”, 96 lbs, 34-C/D chest and small butt. I bought the smallest size. Pros: Very cute. Thick material. It was a bit tight on me and I’m really small. But it wasn’t right enough to return since I liked it for all those other reasons. But for someone taller or bigger than me, the smallest size might be too small. Some of the spots the suit cuts in feel a little weird uncomfortable. Like around my crotch the suit could have cut in a bit more instead of on my thighs, but then on my butt it cuts in a lot and my cheeks feel half exposed. It does show that a bit in the photo but I didn’t really realize it ‘til I put it on. It feels a little weird and bothers me, but I can put swim shorts on over it if I really want. It was still worth buying, but if my butt were covered more comfortably and the suit had a little more stretch, it would be an A+ instead of an A-. Overall happy and would recommend!

I am doing this for all the people like me who shop online and think but how will it look on my body? I’m not built like the models!” So. For posterity,I ordered a size 20W. I am 5’7” and about 208. I am an apple with no butt and small thighs. My girls are 49” at the fullest and underneath 43” around the ribs. For real folks, my hips and waist are both 46”. Truly an apple.But that said, the bottoms overall fit is a bit loose. Great-no camel toe! The legs and butt are neither baggy nor snug and the waist is extremely comfortable BUT, I am not sure how they well they will stay up in water. Doubtful they would stay up with a dive. Overall, the bottoms are great!The top…girls are all snug but with room to breathe, move and not pop out AND they are supported wirelessly! There are soft cup molds in the “bra” but there are strategic holes you can remove them but you risk losing some support. I’m leaving them in. The skirt drapes nicely over the apple belly like a silky curtain (not a tent) and nothing scritchy, scratchy or diggy noticed. The straps are sturdy and don’t dig into my shoulders.You can wear straight strapped as it comes (I got lucky, the adjustment is already perfect and I don’t feel like the straps are going to slide off my shoulders. If you are afraid they will slide you can cross them in back.Crossed tightened them up and I didn’t want to change the adjustment so now there is a bit of digging and the “fluffy overhang”.I hope you find these . I might have to order a second one because I love this!

This swimsuit is the best swimsuit I’ve ever had! The pink color is exactly what I was looking for. Yes, it’s bright…but it somehow isn’t too much. I’m normally an 18W, 2X, 42D and pear shaped with more weight in my hips, lower belly, thighs than across my chest. I’ve been on a weight loss journey, so I may be closer to a 40D or 42C now and can fit in certain 1X tops. I got this suit in a 20W (I normally size up 1 size in swimsuits) and it fits perfect! The material is buttery smooth and very nice to the touch. I thought it wouldn’t look nice across my bust because of the way it’s padded, but it’s actually very flattering! I will likely always wear this with the straps, unless I’m trying to get some sun on my shoulders without having strap tan-lines, but the straps are fully removable. Without the straps it feels as secure as a strapless top can feel. I have a smaller chest, so I’m not sure how it would be without straps if you had a larger chest. The bottoms are not high cut and cover my hips well so my lower belly fat and hip jiggles aren’t exposed at all. The ruching on the front is very attractive and hides imperfections well. The top comes down low enough across my hips to make it look like a one piece. I just love the way I feel in this suit and love that it’s a tankini so I can wear different bottoms if I like and mix and match pieces. My husband loves this suit on me too. Definitely a winner!

Love it. I wear a 2x but got a 3x. Fits perfect. I were a 42DDD I weigh 285lbs and iust shy of 5’8” Freaking love it. It . Not the best stitching But when I find a suite worth in. I’ll buy it every year.

Overall, I like the suit and material. I like that the skirt is stitched down at the hips and won’t float up around the chest like dress suits do so it won’t get in the way. The chest has good support and coverage, though the nipple pad covers move around a lot and scrunch easily I wish there was a easy way to remove them or that they were tacked in place so they wouldn’t move. My biggest issue, which I wanted to share for other plus size ladies looking at this suit. I am 280lb and 5’3” and the shorts can not be used as shorts. When I try to use them as shorts they roll up uncomfortably around the crotch region as shown in the photos with the black lining showing. Nothing I did fixed it. The pattern needs to be fixed to on this part because I have had plus size swim suits that are shorts for my size and work just fine without bunching and rolling up around the thighs and fupa. However, if you pull them up more like a brief you still get better coverage than a true brief so no intimate part slippage and it is still comfortable and no cutting into the skin at least. I do appreciate that there are actual straps and it is not a check tie like my last suit that had a skirt stitched at the hips like this. I gave this 3 stars due to: - Nipple pads move and scrunch a lot with no way to easily remove them without damaging the suit - shorts design is not very functional as shorts for plus size/heavier ladies needs a better pattern cut and stitch design (but not a reason not to purchase - wish the straps had an adjustability to them to make them tighter or looser as needed - back of skirt could be a little longer Overall I do recommend the suit

I love this swim top so much that I have bought it in various sizes as my weight changed. If you will excuse all the messy, headed to donation clothes in the background, the pictures show the suit top from the sides do you can see the side coverage and support you get. I have owned variations of this from 42 DD down to a 34 DDD. Full disclosure, breast cancer reconstruction left me with fake girls that don’t change in size when I do LOL! No matter what my size has been, this top supports like no other I have owned. It has double hooks in back, with three band size options. It is underwired and fully lined, but not padded. The sides come down to midriff level, so you are getting a lot of support at all levels. It also has side boning to mash down some of the goodgie stuff we don’t want to show. If you have any of those under bra rolls or under hang, you know what I mean. From the side, you can see it offers a lot of support. The front looks amazing, but the sides really show how well it holds the ladies up. I do not find that it runs large. I usually wear a 34DD, but in this I think I need to size up to a DDD. Either way, if you like to have a super impressive looking, well supported bust, you will love this. It supports as well or better than any bra I own, including you-know-who’s secret. For the price, you could probably replace some overpriced, under performing bras with this one. This is my go to style, and I know I will be ordering multiple colors. I have worn a variation of this top for probably 10 years. Buying from Amazon gives me the benefit of free shipping without having to order multiple items from the website at one time.

I love (almost) everything about this swimsuit. The print is adorable and the length of the skirt is exactly what I was looking for (hits top of thigh on me). Bust support is good, it basically relies on spandex for support rather than a wire. The girls are locked in place and I have some nice cleavage to boot! Underneath is basically boy-shorts that transition in a mostly sheer bodice, so beware if you're splashing around and aren't comfortable. But please do NOT let that detour from purchasing, it's just an FYI. The only real, almost non, issue for me is sizing. Almost every review mentioned it's true to size. I unfortunately don't find that's true. Like most swimwear I always suggest sizing up. For reference I'm a solid 2 in Torrid and 18/20 in Lane Bryant sizes and I sized up to a 3 here. I'm also 5'9 and about 250 lbs (38D). If I had kept the size 2 in my cart I would've had to return it. So all in all I'm still giving it 5 stars, but beware it might be a little on the small size like most swimsuits are.

This is the most comfortable swimsuit I’ve ever owned. I kinda wish it had a built-in bra, but I’ve been wearing it to daily water aerobics using either a regular lightly padded bralette underneath, which works perfectly fine and holds things together. The straps of the bra can’t be seen thanks to the swim shirt. The shorts are a good length - just above my knee (I’m 5’2”). The whole outfit looks well put together, and I hope the colors will stay as crisp as they are after just two weeks or wearing it daily. Oh, best of all - the top does not float up at all, no matter how much you jump or move around in the water. I’m beginning to eye the colors for a second swimsuit. The color has stayed true in spite of multiple uses. Note: I bought a second swimsuit, in Floral 19. The shirt with a zipper is not as comfortable as the shirts with no zipper. The zipper feels heavy and pulls down on the fabric. While the blue print is lovely, the blue shorts have become faded into several shades of blue/purple. The contrasting yarn at the seams all over the swimsuit turned into an intense red that clashes against the blue of the swimsuit. In summary, I really like and recommend the black swimsuit. I cannot recommend the blue swimsuit. Will lower the stars. .

I'm 5'6" and around 118 pounds. I ordered a size small and it fits really well. The cups are a tad too big, but that's how everything fits me up top (I'm a 30B or 32A for reference). It's not big enough to be a problem. This is the most flattering swimsuit I've ever owned. Even though I'm pretty thin, I do have some belly rolls that I'd rather not flaunt at the beach, and these bottoms have the perfect coverage. The ruching is really flattering. The straps on the top are adjustable and I had to make them pretty small to fit (again, 32A problems), but there's still plenty of tightening that can be done. The ties on the top can be tied a couple of different ways. I like them tied a little lower down on my torso to cover a little more skin, but you could also tie it higher, under the bustline for a more traditional bikini look. I'm seriously considering buying a second one in another color as a backup! It seems well-made. I didn't notice any loose threads or anything like that. The fabric also seemed opaque enough that it won't become transparent when wet. The fabric is nice and stretchy so if I gain weight (always a goal of mine!) I know it will still fit for a while. A lot of times, bikinis in my size are too skimpy for my taste. The bottoms don't even cover my bottom and the top is like two eyepatches on a string. The days where I want to be in public in tiny triangle and string are over. I want coverage now. It's so hard for me to find full coverage swimsuits in my size because I typically have to shop in the juniors' department due to my small size. I was excited to find this one and even more excited that it actually fits pretty well.

My stats: 5’3, 130 lbs, 32DDDD, size 3/5 bottoms, small tops. I’m wearing a size 4. As someone one who owns three summersalt swimsuits, I can confidently say that this is a great alternative to their one shoulder design. The one thing I don’t particularly like about my summersalt swimsuits is the lack of cups. I’ve had three kids, I need the additional coverage that a cup provides or you can clearly see my nipple. That’s not really a look I’m going for when I’m swimming with my kids. I don’t have this issue with this swimsuit. While I prefer the look of the summersalt one shoulder design over this one, mainly because I’m not a huge fan of the bunched fabric look, I feel more confident in this swimsuit. Basically this swimsuit gives me everything I want in swimwear, confidence. While it’s a “hefty” price tag, it’s a quality item, and is in the similar notice range to summersalt.

I really like this swimsuit a lot. It’s comfortable and hugs my curves in the right way. I love how the leotard of the suit is squared at the bottom like shorts almost because it helps keep my tummy in and covers more. I like to buy one piece dresses because of the fact that I hate showing my upper legs in a swimsuit as well as cover my tummy bump. I also lost a ton of weight due to stress so I have an issue with sagging/hanging stomach because of how I lost the weight and didn’t tone so I was happy this swimsuit was able to keep it tucked in. At first when I tried it on I was a little nervous about what would happen if it got wet and I had to use the bathroom as one pieces are so hard to get back on if you’re wet and this fit my body so perfectly so much that I was worried it would be too hard, but I was amazed it was not a problem. It feels like it’s not stretchy enough but it is. The sizing chart was a little confusing and the reviews made it even more confusing because different people were saying different things but I was able to figure out the right size. Normally I’m a 22/24 sometimes a 20 depending on the item/store/material but typically 22/24. I can fit into a 2x at regular stores although I tend to go with the 3x for extra room in my tummy area. I ordered the 4x and it fit perfectly. I do have 2 issues with the suit that one of them most people are saying and it is that it is not really great for swimming in an ocean with huge waves if you are big chested. I am an F cup and let’s just say I had to adjust the girls every time a huge wave came and was practically spilling out at points. But it’s a constant struggle anyway so I just dealt with it. The other thing is and I didn’t realize this until I got to the beach and this is on me and not the suit itself but I should have rethought the suit because of the straps. They crisscross in the back and although they were supportive I didn’t like the giant unevenly thick tan lines I got and I burned pretty bad because the person putting on sunscreen on my back didn’t get that area well. Also it made wearing a bra hard for a few days because of the burn. Normally regular straps that go straight back would have created a neutral spot for my bra straps to rest but because of the cross cross design the part of my back where my bra straps went was burned and I couldn’t wear a bra for a few days. Again, not a flaw on the suit but just worth mentioning to think about. Overall I really like this suit. It’s flattering and cute and is perfect for covering my problem areas but I think I may look into a different suit for the beach next time and save this one for the pool. Or find someone else to put on sunscreen for me lol I’ll post more pics in it another time. I didn’t take full body pics yet

I have been searching for a simple one piece to avoid sunburn without having tons of extra fabric to hold water like most larger suits have. I'm 5'4" and about 360lbs and the 6X is clearly made for someone taller, but it fits fine. (I think it might even work for someone a bit heavier than me) I like that it comes up very high in the armpit so I'm less likely to get a sunburn on that area. It's just a plain, cheaply made bathing suit, but it's comfortable and functional. I do wish the gusset were wider, and I would prefer the leg holes be cut a bit lower, and the back higher, but it's not a deal breaker. I will say that the cups had just been folded in half and shoved in, so they're winked and they're difficult to get placed correctly. They're also thin, so you may want a dark color if you're worried about people noticing your nipples. There's also some uneven sewing and a bit of a weird spot where the bra shelf connects to the side. Overall, I'm just thrilled this suit exists in this size and I'm quite happy with it after one day in the pool.

I don't know if they will ride up much with time, but they are nice. The tie in the front is a nice feature. Just know if you do tie it up it will pull the front of the top up a bit. I bought a size Medium and it fits just right. I do have a belly pooch and as you can see it covers it nicely. I like that it covers the backside well too. Overall I feel this suit is slimming and attractive. Time will tell how well they hold up for the price.
